summary: Tight first set, soaring second‑set jams with standout Stagger Lee and Terrapin
blurb: The band delivers a tight, energetic first set highlighted by Stagger Lee, It’s All Over Now and Row Jimmy. The second set builds momentum with a powerful Terrapin Station into Playing In The Band suite, featuring stellar Samson & Delilah and an emotive Black Peter. Brent’s Hammond work clicks in nicely and the humor of a monitor‑buzz moment adds charm.
review:
May 3, 1979 at Charlotte Coliseum finds the Dead firing on all cylinders. The first set rolls out with crisp, punchy renditions – Jerry’s guitar is blazing on Stagger Lee and Samson & Delilah, while Bob’s vocals are described as “impressive, virile” on It’s All Over Now and Row Jimmy. A brief monitor‑buzz mishap adds a laugh‑filled interlude that the band rides with good humor.

The second set is where the night really shines. Terrapin Station segues seamlessly into a soaring Playing In The Band, leading through drums, space, and a heartfelt Black Peter before looping back into another explosive Playing In The Band climax. Brent’s Hammond organ finally gels with the group, providing rich harmonies that lift the jam‑conversations, and Jerry’s lead work is “shredded” on Samson & Delilah and emotionally resonant on Black Peter. Overall, despite a few uneven moments in the first set, the show captures the Dead at a creative high point with strong second‑set momentum and plenty of memorable moments.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive, praising the tight first set and strong second‑set jams; a third is mixed but still notes several highlights. Consistent praise for standout songs (Stagger Lee, Samson & Delilah, Terrapin Station) and overall energetic performance yields a solid positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Clear audience tape with good detail, captures the tight first set and strong second‑set jams; minimal crowd noise and no major dropouts.

key_highlights: Tight, energetic first set with standout Stagger Lee, It’s All Over Now and Row Jimmy
Second‑set momentum builds through Terrapin Station → Playing In The Band suite, ending with an emotive Black Peter
Humorous monitor‑buzz incident adds character to the night
Brent’s Hammond organ clicks in, providing rich harmonies that lift the jams

band_performance.Jerry: Blazing lead work throughout – shredding on Samson & Delilah, fiery solo on Stagger Lee, and an emotional delivery on Black Peter; consistently praised for his guitar chatter.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation with a few light‑hearted jokes; noted for conversational interplay with Jerry and Bob during the jams.
band_performance.Bob: Impressive, virile vocals on key numbers; strong interplay with Jerry especially in the Big River jam and handled the monitor buzz with good humor.
band_performance.Brent: Hammond organ and harmonies are a highlight, marking a clear improvement over earlier keyboard eras; while one source mentions tentative moments, overall his playing gels well with the band.
band_performance.Mickey: Drumming is steady and patient, providing reliable support without drawing negative criticism.
band_performance.Billy: Provides standard rhythmic backbone; noted for walking around during extended jams but no major issues mentioned.
